metadata.triage_system: START (Simple Triage and Rapid Treatment) + Indian EMS P1/P2/P3
metadata.ground_truth_basis: AHA ACLS Guidelines 2020, MCI START Protocol, NEM India EMS Standards
metadata.note: Each template has a deterministic ground truth used by graders. Symptom text is fed to agent as natural language.

P2.label: Urgent
P2.description: Serious but stable, can wait short period

templates.call_description: 58-year-old male, sudden severe crushing chest pain radiating to left arm and jaw, started 25 minutes ago. Sweating profusely, feels like elephant sitting on chest. BP 90/60, pulse 48 irregular. Wife says he looks grey.
templates.protocol_notes: STEMI with cardiogenic shock indicators. MICU mandatory — requires 12-lead ECG en route, aspirin 325mg, nitrate if BP allows, cath lab activation. Closest facility with cath lab.

templates.call_description: 65-year-old diabetic woman, vomiting, upper abdominal pain, unusual fatigue for 2 hours. No chest pain reported. Neighbour called 108. Pulse 52 bpm, diaphoretic, BP 100/65.
templates.protocol_notes: Atypical STEMI presentation in diabetic female — epigastric symptoms without chest pain are classic masquerade. High index of suspicion required. MICU for 12-lead, cath lab activation.

templates.call_description: 72-year-old retired teacher, sudden onset chest tightness, collapsed in garden. Bystander CPR in progress. No pulse felt by caller. History of hypertension.
templates.protocol_notes: STEMI-precipitated VF arrest. MICU with defib. Resuscitate en route, cath lab on standby. Every minute of VF = 10% survival decrease.

templates.call_description: 49-year-old smoker, chest pain 3/10 for past 4 hours, now 8/10 suddenly. Shortness of breath, light-headed. Sitting at desk in office. HR 110, BP 145/90.
templates.protocol_notes: Classic STEMI. Escalating chest pain in male smoker. MICU mandatory.

templates.call_description: 67-year-old woman, sudden drooping of left side of face, arm weakness, slurred speech, started 45 minutes ago. FAST positive. BP 185/110, alert, confused.
templates.protocol_notes: Acute ischemic stroke, thrombolysis window open. FAST positive. ALS with pre-notification to stroke unit. Door-to-needle target 60 min. No CT required before transport.

templates.call_description: 55-year-old hypertensive male, thunderclap headache worst of life, neck stiffness, vomiting, photophobia. Found on bathroom floor. BP 220/130, GCS 12.
templates.protocol_notes: Subarachnoid hemorrhage — thunderclap headache is pathognomonic. ALS, BP management en route, neurosurgery centre mandatory. No thrombolytics.

templates.call_description: 78-year-old, woke up with right-sided weakness and unable to speak. Symptom onset unknown — last seen normal at 10pm. Atrial fibrillation history. GCS 13.
templates.protocol_notes: Wake-up stroke with unknown onset. MRI-DWI FLAIR mismatch will determine eligibility. ALS + stroke unit with advanced imaging capability.

templates.call_description: High-speed head-on collision on Mumbai-Pune Expressway. 34-year-old driver unresponsive, airbag deployed, significant intrusion into cabin. Blood visible from multiple sites. Pulse weak and rapid, RR 28.
templates.protocol_notes: Polytrauma with entrapment. Fire extrication mandatory before EMS access. Police scene safety. MICU for RSI-capable crew. Level 1 trauma centre only. Permissive hypotension strategy.

templates.call_description: Pedestrian vs truck on highway. 22-year-old male, ejected 10 metres, not moving. Multiple obvious deformities limbs. Bystanders say no pulse for 30 seconds then returned. GCS 6. RR 32.
templates.protocol_notes: High-mechanism pedestrian strike. Traumatic cardiac arrest with ROSC. MICU, level 1 trauma, activate massive transfusion protocol.

templates.call_description: Construction site collapse. Worker fell 4 floors. Pelvis visibly deformed, abdomen distended and rigid. GCS 10. BP 80/50, HR 130, RR 24. Groaning.
templates.protocol_notes: Unstable pelvis with intra-abdominal hemorrhage. Immediate pelvic binder, massive transfusion. Level 1 trauma, immediate OR.

templates.call_description: 32-year-old asthmatic, severe bronchospasm, not responding to home nebuliser after 4 doses. Cannot complete sentences, sits forward, using neck muscles to breathe. SpO2 82% on room air.
templates.protocol_notes: Status asthmaticus with imminent respiratory failure. ALS for salbutamol nebulisation, Mg sulphate IV, possible RSI. Nearest ED with ICU capability.

templates.call_description: 75-year-old COPD patient, severe breathlessness, audible wheeze, cyanotic lips. Cannot lie flat. Confused and agitated. Caregiver says this is worse than any previous episode. RR 38.
templates.protocol_notes: Acute COPD exacerbation with type 2 respiratory failure. ALS — controlled oxygen (target 88-92%), bronchodilators, NIV preparation. Hospital with ventilator capability.

templates.call_description: 6-month-old infant, fast breathing, poor feeding for 2 days, now bluish around lips. Temperature 38.8C. Intercostal recessions visible. Parents panicked.
templates.protocol_notes: Severe bronchiolitis/pneumonia in infant with cyanosis. ALS for paediatric airway management. Paediatric emergency centre mandatory.

templates.call_description: LPG cylinder explosion in kitchen. 40-year-old woman, 60% body surface area burns, facial involvement, singed eyebrows, hoarse voice. Screaming. HR 130, BP 110/80.
templates.protocol_notes: Major burns with inhalation injury suspected (hoarse voice, singed eyebrows). ALS for early airway — intubate before oedema closes airway. Burns unit mandatory. Parkland formula IV fluids.

templates.call_description: Diwali firecracker accident. 12-year-old boy, 25% BSA burns to hands, forearms, and face. Alert and crying. No respiratory distress.
templates.protocol_notes: Paediatric burn 25% BSA. ALS for IV access and analgesia. Burns unit needed, paediatric burns preferred.

templates.call_description: Electrical burn. Electrician touched live wire in panel room. Entry wound right hand, exit wound left foot. Alert, no cardiac symptoms. HR 82, BP 120/80.
templates.protocol_notes: Electrical burn with possible cardiac arrhythmia risk. Continuous cardiac monitoring. ALS mandatory. Burns unit for deep tissue assessment. Scene safety — ensure power is OFF.

templates.call_description: 28-year-old woman, 34 weeks pregnant, sudden severe headache, visual disturbances, and swelling face and hands. BP 175/115 by husband. Baby kicking. No contractions yet.
templates.protocol_notes: Severe pre-eclampsia approaching eclampsia. ALS for IV Mg sulphate prophylaxis, antihypertensives. Obstetric centre with NICU capability.

templates.call_description: 32-year-old, 38 weeks, heavy vaginal bleeding, severe abdominal pain, rigid abdomen. Husband says she is pale and drowsy. History of placenta praevia. HR 128, BP 80/50.
templates.protocol_notes: Placental abruption with hemorrhagic shock. MICU — large bore IV, rapid fluid, blood group and save. Immediate obstetric surgical team. Massive hemorrhage protocol.

templates.call_description: 24-year-old, first pregnancy, 40 weeks, active labour at home. Water broke 3 hours ago. Contractions every 2 minutes. Husband is a driver, no female family nearby.
templates.protocol_notes: Imminent normal delivery. BLS with trained birth attendant. Any hospital with obstetrics. Prepare for en-route delivery.

templates.call_description: 68-year-old diabetic, fever 39.8C for 3 days, now confused and barely responsive. Rapid breathing 34/min. BP 75/40. Family says urine dark, no urination for 6 hours. Cold clammy extremities.
templates.protocol_notes: Septic shock — Surviving Sepsis Campaign bundle. ALS for IV access, vasopressors if available, blood cultures before antibiotics if possible. ICU facility.

templates.call_description: 55-year-old post-surgical patient from private hospital, fever, wound dehiscence, foul smell from abdomen incision. RR 26, HR 105, BP 105/70, confused.
templates.protocol_notes: Surgical site sepsis with SIRS. Early goal directed therapy. Return to surgical facility preferred.

templates.call_description: 26-year-old woman, bee sting 10 minutes ago, throat swelling rapidly, difficulty swallowing, hives all over body, wheezing, BP 70/40. Husband says she never had allergy before.
templates.protocol_notes: Severe anaphylaxis with impending airway loss. ALS — IM adrenaline 0.5mg IMMEDIATELY, IV access, fluid, antihistamine, steroid, prepare for surgical airway. Any emergency capable hospital.

templates.call_description: 19-year-old, ate peanuts unknowingly in restaurant, known peanut allergy. Used own epipen. Some improvement but still wheezing and throat tight. BP 95/60. Alert.
templates.protocol_notes: Biphasic anaphylaxis risk after partial epipen response. ALS, second dose adrenaline, IV access, minimum 6 hours observation. Any ED.

templates.call_description: 12-year-old boy, submerged in pond for unknown duration, bystanders pulled him out. Not breathing, no pulse. CPR being performed by villager.
templates.protocol_notes: Paediatric drowning with cardiac arrest. ALS. Continue CPR, warm if hypothermic — don't pronounce until warm and dead. Paediatric ICU.

templates.call_description: Monsoon flood rescue. 45-year-old man recovered from floodwater, coughing, wheezing, alert. SpO2 88%. Shivering. Minor laceration on leg.
templates.protocol_notes: Near-drowning with aspiration pneumonitis risk. BLS with O2. Observation for delayed pulmonary oedema. General ED acceptable.

templates.call_description: Farmer, 52 years, drank organophosphate pesticide intentionally 30 minutes ago. Excessive secretions, small pupils, muscle twitching, defecating involuntarily. RR 36, HR 42, BP 90/60.
templates.protocol_notes: SLUDGE syndrome — classic organophosphate. ALS for atropine (LARGE doses 2-4mg IV, repeat every 5 min), pralidoxime. PPE for crew — skin contamination risk. Toxicology unit.

templates.call_description: 18-year-old college student, party setting, unresponsive on bathroom floor. Friends say she consumed unknown tablets plus alcohol. RR 6, pupils pinpoint, cyanotic. Pulse present.
templates.protocol_notes: Opioid overdose — classic triad. ALS: naloxone 0.4-2mg IV/IN, bag-mask ventilation, airway management. Any ED with ICU.

templates.call_description: Chemical factory leak, Raigad MIDC. Worker exposed to chlorine gas, eyes streaming, throat burning, coughing violently, in respiratory distress. 3 workers affected. HR 118.
templates.protocol_notes: Chlorine gas inhalation — mass casualty potential. Decontamination before hospital entry. Fire hazmat team for zone control. ALS for bronchodilators, O2. Toxicology centre.

templates.call_description: 3-year-old girl, swallowed button battery 2 hours ago. Now crying inconsolably, drooling, refusing food. Parents found empty battery tray. RR 28, SpO2 96%.
templates.protocol_notes: Button battery ingestion — within 2 hours of esophageal battery, severe tissue liquefaction can occur. ALS. Paediatric surgical emergency. No eating/drinking.

templates.call_description: 7-year-old boy, febrile seizure, still convulsing after 8 minutes. Temperature 40.2C. Not stopping. Parents terrified. Pale, shaking all limbs.
templates.protocol_notes: Status epilepticus >5 min. ALS for rectal/buccal diazepam or IV lorazepam. Airway protection. Paediatric emergency centre.

templates.call_description: 28-year-old motorcyclist, fell at slow speed in parking lot. Helmet worn. Grazes on both hands, limping, left ankle swollen but able to weight-bear with difficulty. Alert, normal vitals.
templates.protocol_notes: Minor trauma. BLS. Nearest ED. X-ray for ankle assessment.

templates.call_description: Office worker, 35, tripped on stairs, hit head on railing. Brief LOC (5 seconds per witness), now alert, mild headache, small scalp laceration. GCS 15, BP 125/80.
templates.protocol_notes: Minor head injury with LOC. BLS. CT head indicated. Watch for deterioration. ED with CT capability.

templates.call_description: 58-year-old pedestrian hit by auto-rickshaw at 30kmph. Alert and talking. Pain right thigh, obvious deformity femur. BP 105/70, HR 102. No other obvious injuries.
templates.protocol_notes: Femur fracture with tachycardia — potential 1-2L blood loss in thigh. ALS for IV access, analgesia, traction splint. Orthopaedic capable hospital.

templates.call_description: Stabbing victim, 24-year-old male. Single stab wound left chest, alert, BP 100/70, HR 118, RR 24. Decreased breath sounds left side. Sat 91%.
templates.protocol_notes: Penetrating chest trauma with haemopneumothorax. ALS — needle decompression if tension PTX, occlusive dressing. Police for scene security. Level 1 trauma, immediate surgical team.

templates.call_description: 42-year-old runner collapsed at 5km mark of marathon, bystander CPR ongoing. AED shocked once. Back to CPR. HR undetectable. Not breathing.
templates.protocol_notes: Witnessed VF cardiac arrest with AED shock and CPR — high survival potential. MICU for advanced resuscitation, consider cath lab post-ROSC. Every minute without CPR = 10% less survival.

templates.call_description: 70-year-old woman found unresponsive by family. Uncertain downtime. Not breathing. No pulse. DNR form found in house. Family member is requesting resuscitation anyway.
templates.protocol_notes: Cardiac arrest with valid DNR. BLS to attend, confirm DNR, comfort the family. No resuscitation. Ethical obligation to honour advance directive.

templates.call_description: 50-year-old office worker, found slumped at desk. Colleagues doing CPR. Unknown downtime, last seen well 20 minutes ago. AED says no shock advised.
templates.protocol_notes: PEA arrest, 20-min downtime. MICU for H's and T's assessment, adrenaline, continued CPR. Hospital with cardiac and ICU capability.

templates.call_description: 24-year-old male on rooftop, threatening to jump. Agitated, pacing. Family says he stopped his medication 2 weeks ago. Police already present. No physical injury.
templates.protocol_notes: Psychiatric emergency with suicidal threat. ALS + Police. Do not approach until scene declared safe. Sedation may be needed post-rescue. Psychiatric facility or ED.

templates.call_description: 42-year-old insulin-dependent diabetic, found by family unconscious, diaphoretic. Known to skip meals. GCS 8, RR 14, HR 88, BP 130/85. No facial droop.
templates.protocol_notes: Severe hypoglycaemia with altered consciousness. ALS for IV dextrose 50% 50mL, glucagon 1mg IM if no IV access. GCS should recover rapidly if hypoglycaemia. If not — reassess for stroke.

templates.call_description: 8-year-old type 1 diabetic, school nurse called. Child confused, shaking, pale. Blood glucose 1.9 mmol/L by school glucometer. Alert but not cooperating.
templates.protocol_notes: Paediatric hypoglycaemia, child can swallow. BLS — oral dextrose gel if cooperative, IV if not. Paediatric ED for observation.

templates.call_description: Motorcycle crash at 80kmph. 25-year-old, helmet came off on impact. Complaining of severe neck pain, cannot move hands or feel legs. Alert, talking. BP 90/50, HR 52 (paradoxical bradycardia).
templates.protocol_notes: C-spine injury with neurogenic shock (bradycardia + hypotension). MICU for vasopressors, spinal immobilisation with log-roll technique, MRI-capable neurosurgery centre. Steroids controversial — follow local protocol.

templates.call_description: 55-year-old fell from roof during repair work. Back pain, lower limb weakness, loss of bladder control. Alert. HR 82, BP 120/80. Paraesthesia bilateral legs.
templates.protocol_notes: Thoracolumbar spinal injury with incomplete cord lesion. ALS, spinal precautions. Neurosurgery for decompression. Time-sensitive — incomplete lesion may recover with early intervention.

templates.call_description: Labour worker at construction site, May afternoon 44°C. Collapsed. Skin hot and dry, not sweating despite heat. Confused, GCS 10. Temperature 41.5°C. HR 124, BP 95/60.
templates.protocol_notes: Classic heat stroke — NO sweating, altered consciousness. ALS — RAPID cooling (ice packs groin/axilla/neck, cool IV fluids, fan). Target < 39°C within 30 min. Any ICU-capable ED.

templates.call_description: Wari pilgrimage, 80-year-old. Exhaustion, dizziness, nausea. Skin pale and sweaty. Temperature 38.2°C. Alert, talking. BP 100/70 on sitting, 80/55 on standing.
templates.protocol_notes: Heat exhaustion (not stroke — still sweating, <40°C). BLS — oral/IV rehydration, shade, cooling. ED observation.

templates.call_description: Bus vs truck collision on NH65. Caller reports 15-20 injured, 3-4 not moving. Bus overturned. Multiple victims with varying injuries. Scene not yet safe — fuel leaking.
templates.protocol_notes: MCI — declare mass casualty. Request multiple units (MICU + ALS + BLS). START triage on scene. Fire for fuel/entrapment, Police for traffic and scene. Distribute to multiple Level 1 trauma centres.

templates.call_description: Boiler explosion at textile factory, Bhiwandi. 25+ workers injured. Smoke and dust everywhere. Burns, blast injuries, possible crush. Workers still inside the building.
templates.protocol_notes: MCI with blast + burn mechanism. Multiple MICU + ALS. Fire for structural safety clearance. Blast injury triad: barotrauma (ears, lungs), fragmentation, blunt. Burns unit mandatory for burns casualties.

templates.call_description: Ganesh Chaturthi immersion festival. Young man, 21, drowned during procession. Bystanders CPR. Estimated 5-minute submersion in river. Full arrest.
templates.protocol_notes: Freshwater drowning cardiac arrest. ALS. Continue CPR 30:2, warm if hypothermic, ventilate. Any hospital with ICU. Do not declare dead until warm and unresponsive.

templates.call_description: 22-year-old farmer, bitten on right foot by snake while working in field, Vidarbha region. Foot swelling rapidly, severe pain, petechiae on leg. Drooling starting. Nausea. BP 90/60.
templates.protocol_notes: Russell's viper envenomation — hemotoxic pattern. ALS, IV ASV (polyvalent anti-snake venom) 8-10 vials IV ASAP. Do NOT cut/suck/tourniquet. Hospital with ASV stock. Vidarbha snakebite centre preferred.

templates.call_description: Cobra bite, rural Nashik. 45-year-old, 30 minutes ago, right hand. Drowsy, difficulty breathing, dropping eyelids (ptosis). HR 60, BP 100/70.
templates.protocol_notes: Neurotoxic cobra envenomation — ptosis and respiratory paralysis. ALS for airway/ventilation. ASV IV immediately, neostigmine if atropine test positive. Any hospital with ventilator.

templates.call_description: 62-year-old chronic renal failure patient, missed 2 dialysis sessions. Severe breathlessness, confusion, HR 52, peaked T-waves on portable monitor. K+ likely high. BP 175/110.
templates.protocol_notes: Hyperkalaemia with ECG changes — cardiac arrest risk. MICU: IV calcium gluconate 10mL immediately (membrane stabilisation), then glucose+insulin, salbutamol nebulisation. Dialysis centre mandatory.

templates.call_description: 35-year-old male, sudden severe epigastric pain radiating to back, worst of life. History of alcohol use. Board-like rigid abdomen. Temperature 37.8C. HR 115, BP 100/70.
templates.protocol_notes: Acute severe pancreatitis vs perforated viscus. ALS, IV fluids aggressively, analgesia. Surgical hospital with ICU.

templates.call_description: 70-year-old woman, 3 days constipated, now sudden pain left iliac fossa, fever 38.9°C. Abdomen guarded. No bowel sounds. HR 108, BP 105/75. Looks unwell.
templates.protocol_notes: Perforated diverticulum / bowel obstruction with peritonitis. ALS, IV fluids, antibiotics, surgical hospital.

templates.call_description: 10-year-old girl fell from bicycle, crying. Skin grazed on knee and palm. Alert, walking, no deformity, normal neuro exam. Scared but physically well.
templates.protocol_notes: Minor paediatric trauma. BLS. Wound care at any ED. Reassurance for child and parent.

templates.call_description: 16-year-old girl, laceration both wrists, superficial, not bleeding profusely. Alert, crying, says she wants to die. Parents present and distraught.
templates.protocol_notes: Self-harm with suicidal ideation. BLS for wound care. ED with psychiatric liaison. Do not leave alone. Safeguarding.

templates.call_description: Younger patient, 38 years, cocaine use history admitted by wife. Chest pain onset during argument. Diaphoretic, HR 138 and irregular, BP 160/100.
templates.protocol_notes: Cocaine-induced vasospasm STEMI. AVOID beta-blockers. Benzodiazepines for agitation. Cath lab activation. MICU.

templates.call_description: 68-year-old male, hypertensive, sudden tearing chest/back pain radiating to both legs. Unequal BP in arms (R 180/110, L 120/80). Legs feel numb. HR 110.
templates.protocol_notes: Type A aortic dissection — surgical emergency. MICU. BP control (target SBP 100-120), heart rate control. Do NOT give thrombolytics. Cardiothoracic surgical centre.

templates.call_description: 2-year-old, accidental ingestion of household kerosene — maid saw it happen. Child coughing violently, blue-tinged lips, drooling. 10 minutes ago.
templates.protocol_notes: Hydrocarbon aspiration — DO NOT induce vomiting (aspiration risk). ALS for airway, O2. Paediatric ED with ICU. Decontaminate clothing.

templates.call_description: 55-year-old male, agricultural accident. Arm caught in threshing machine. Traumatic amputation right forearm at mid-shaft. Tourniquet applied by farm workers. Alert, BP 110/75, HR 98.
templates.protocol_notes: Traumatic amputation with tourniquet. ALS — verify tourniquet effectiveness, IV access, analgesia. Bring amputated part in saline-moist sterile dressing inside ice bag (NOT directly on ice). Replantation centre within 6 hours.

templates.call_description: 45-year-old COVID pneumonia patient at home, SpO2 dropped to 68% on 5L home oxygen. Severely breathless at rest. Cannot speak full sentences. Cyanotic.
templates.protocol_notes: Severe hypoxia — ARDS/COVID pneumonia. ALS for high-flow O2, prone positioning if tolerated, prepare for intubation. ICU with ventilator. Isolate crew with full PPE.

templates.call_description: Newborn, delivered at home by untrained attendant 30 minutes ago. Not crying, limp, bluish, not breathing. Mother is unresponsive from blood loss.
templates.protocol_notes: Dual emergency — neonatal arrest AND maternal haemorrhage. ALS for neonatal resuscitation (NRP protocol). Second unit for mother. NICU + obstetric surgical hospital.

templates.call_description: 25-year-old woman, 12 weeks pregnant, fainted in a queue, fell forward. No head injury. Small bruise on knee. Recovering consciousness, GCS 15, fetal movement felt.
templates.protocol_notes: Syncope with fall in pregnancy. BLS. Obstetric ED — CTG monitoring for foetal wellbeing. Minor injury. Rule out placental abruption.

templates.call_description: Earthquake aftershock — building partial collapse, Latur district. 42-year-old man, leg trapped under rubble for 2 hours. Alert but deteriorating. HR 122, BP 85/55. Swollen dusky leg.
templates.protocol_notes: Crush syndrome — IV fluids BEFORE and DURING extrication (pre-treat rhabdomyolysis), alkalinise urine. MICU for haemodynamics. Fire extrication mandatory. Renal dialysis facility — will develop ARF.

templates.call_description: 5-year-old girl, peanut lodged in throat by history, now severe stridor, drooling, cannot swallow. Sat 90%. Clutching throat. Mother hysterical.
templates.protocol_notes: Severe paediatric airway obstruction — DO NOT do blind finger sweep. Try 5 back blows + 5 abdominal thrusts if child responsive. If unconscious, CPR. ALS for surgical airway if all fail. Immediate paediatric ED.

templates.call_description: 22-year-old athlete, suddenly felt pounding irregular heartbeat during match. Now lightheaded. ECG shows WPW pattern on teammate's smartwatch. HR 220, BP 80/50.
templates.protocol_notes: WPW with SVT — AVOID adenosine, beta blockers, calcium channel blockers (can cause VF). MICU — DC cardioversion if haemodynamically unstable. Electrophysiology capable hospital.

templates.call_description: 45-year-old woman, domestic violence. Deep laceration right cheek, 4 bruised ribs, BP 130/85, HR 92. GCS 15. Alert. Refuses to give details about attacker.
templates.protocol_notes: Domestic violence victim. BLS. Safe transport — do not leave with potential attacker. Police notification mandatory. ED with social worker. Forensic documentation.

templates.call_description: 60-year-old male, 6 hours post-surgery (laparoscopic appendicectomy), now breathless, pleuritic chest pain, right leg swollen and tender. HR 112, SpO2 89%, BP 130/90.
templates.protocol_notes: Pulmonary embolism post-op — Wells score very high. ALS, O2, IV access. CT-PA at hospital. Anticoagulation or thrombolysis decision. Any hospital with CT and haematology.

templates.call_description: Industrial accident — 3 workers overcome by carbon monoxide from diesel generator running indoors at construction site. All confused. HR 108, SpO2 reads 98% (false). Headache, nausea.
templates.protocol_notes: CO poisoning — SpO2 unreliable. ALS, 100% O2 via non-rebreather mask. Scene ventilation by Fire. HBO if available — dramatically shortens CO half-life. ABG for carboxyhaemoglobin.

templates.call_description: 35-year-old, 32 weeks, road traffic accident as passenger. Seat belt was worn. Complains of abdominal pain and uterine tightenings. Fetal movement reduced. BP 120/80, HR 94.
templates.protocol_notes: Trauma in pregnancy. ALS — left lateral tilt to avoid aortocaval compression. CTG monitoring essential. Obstetric hospital with NICU. Fetal monitoring for 4 hours minimum.

templates.call_description: 4-year-old male, measles rash for 5 days, now suddenly limp, high fever 40.5C, altered consciousness, stiff neck. Petechiae developing on trunk. GCS 10.
templates.protocol_notes: Meningococcal septicaemia with possible meningitis. ALS — IV benzylpenicillin 1.2g immediately (do not wait). Paediatric ICU. Non-blanching rash = emergency.

templates.call_description: Cliff road accident on Malshej Ghat — SUV fell 30m down hillside. Driver alert, 3 passengers, 1 unresponsive. Road inaccessible. Helicopter required. 2 hours post-accident.
templates.protocol_notes: Remote MCI, helicopter extraction needed. MICU for advanced resuscitation on arrival. Fire for rope rescue. Police for road closure. Level 1 trauma — 2h downtime increases mortality.

templates.call_description: Acid attack victim. 27-year-old woman, acid thrown on face, neck, and upper chest. Screaming in agony. Skin sloughing. HR 128. Airway patent but edema developing.
templates.protocol_notes: Chemical acid burns — IRRIGATE copiously with water immediately. ALS for airway management before edema progresses. Burns/plastic surgery centre. Police for crime scene. Do NOT neutralise acid with alkali.

templates.call_description: 75-year-old, advanced cancer, palliative care patient. Found by family in bed, not breathing, no pulse. Family expected this. No DNR found at home.
templates.protocol_notes: Expected palliative death. BLS to confirm death, support family. Without DNR, consider 1 cycle of CPR for legal clarity then cease if no ROSC. Doctor for death certification.

templates.call_description: 30-year-old woman, post-childbirth (home delivery 5 days ago). High fever, foul-smelling vaginal discharge, severe abdominal pain, confusion. HR 122, BP 78/48, RR 30.
templates.protocol_notes: Puerperal septic shock — ALS, aggressive IV fluids, blood cultures, IV antibiotics (ceftriaxone + metronidazole). Obstetric + ICU hospital.

templates.call_description: 60-year-old arthritis patient, slipped in bathroom, hip pain, cannot stand. Limb externally rotated and shortened. Alert, stable vitals. No other injuries.
templates.protocol_notes: Neck of femur fracture — high mortality in elderly if delayed. BLS with pain management. Orthopaedic hospital, surgical fixation within 36 hours target.

templates.call_description: Mass food poisoning — temple feast. 40+ people vomiting and having diarrhoea after eating prasad. 3 elderly with confusion, 1 hypotensive. Botulism suspected.
templates.protocol_notes: Suspected botulism mass outbreak. MICU for critical patients. ALS + BLS for moderate. Alert health authorities, quarantine food. Antitoxin from state central store. Mass casualty activation.

templates.call_description: Construction worker, 30, fell 1.5 metres from scaffolding onto soft mud. Lower back pain, walking slowly but able to bear weight. Alert, normal vitals. No radiation to legs.
templates.protocol_notes: Minor spinal mechanism with no neurology. BLS, spinal precautions as standard. X-ray at ED. Low priority.

templates.call_description: Cyclone Nisarga aftermath, Ratnagiri coast. Multiple collapsed structures. Coastal hospital reporting 60+ casualties arriving. Zone has no power, roads cut off in 2 directions. Requesting state mutual aid.
templates.protocol_notes: Natural disaster MCI. State mutual aid mandatory. NDRF coordination. Air evacuation for critical patients. Surge declaration. Distribute patients across regional hospitals — local capacity overwhelmed.

templates.call_description: Nagpur summer, 47°C. Marathon event. 68-year-old spectator, not athlete. Found collapsed behind crowd barrier. Dry hot skin. GCS 8. Temperature 41.8°C. HR 132, BP 80/50.
templates.protocol_notes: Exertional heat stroke in elder — hot dry skin, GCS 8. ALS for cold IV fluids 500mL rapidly, ice packs all sites, fan. Target <39°C in 30 min. ICU for rhabdomyolysis monitoring.

templates.call_description: 25-year-old, 16 weeks pregnant, sudden collapse at home. Severe lower abdominal pain on right side for 2 hours. Pallor, dizziness. HR 128, BP 72/48. US done 3 weeks ago — normal IUD.
templates.protocol_notes: Ruptured ectopic pregnancy — haemoperitoneum. MICU. Rapid IV access, O-negative blood if available, immediate surgical hospital. Fatal within minutes without surgery.

templates.call_description: Sports field: 19-year-old cricketer, direct blow to abdomen from hard ball at 140kmph. Localised left upper quadrant pain, guarding. HR 108, BP 100/72, sat 97%. Alert.
templates.protocol_notes: Splenic injury — tachycardia is ominous. ALS, IV access, potential non-operative or operative management. Surgical centre with blood bank. CT abdomen on arrival.

templates.call_description: 45-year-old HIV-positive male on ART, sudden severe headache, neck rigidity, fever 38.5°C, photophobia. Confused. No focal neurology. CD4 count reportedly very low.
templates.protocol_notes: Cryptococcal meningitis in AIDS — urgent lumbar puncture for diagnosis. ALS, infection control. HIV specialist facility. Amphotericin B if confirmed.

templates.call_description: 82-year-old man, pacemaker patient, suddenly dizzy and fainted briefly. Back to normal. Shows pacemaker card. HR 36 on neighbour's finger pulse check. BP 100/70. Alert now.
templates.protocol_notes: Pacemaker failure — transcutaneous pacing may be needed. ALS with external pacemaker, atropine 0.5mg IV. Cardiology capable hospital. Bring pacemaker ID card.

templates.call_description: 10-month-old baby, accidental fall from bed. Anterior fontanelle bulging, child listless, vomiting 3 times. No bruises visible. Parents unsure of exact fall height. GCS 12.
templates.protocol_notes: Non-accidental injury must be considered — bulging fontanelle, mechanism inconsistent. ALS. Paediatric neurosurgery. Safeguarding alert. Do not leave child with parents alone.

templates.call_description: 72-year-old male with aortic aneurysm known, severe sudden back pain, collapse. Pulsatile abdominal mass. BP 70/40, HR 130. Pale and sweaty. GCS 14.
templates.protocol_notes: Ruptured AAA — 80% mortality without immediate surgery. MICU. Permissive hypotension (SBP 70-90 until clamp in OR). Vascular surgical hospital, immediate theatre. Do NOT give large volumes — raises BP, dislodges clot.

templates.call_description: 35-year-old woman, allergic reaction to medication — urticaria on arms and torso, mild itching. No throat swelling, no breathing difficulty. BP 120/80, HR 80. Alert. Ate antihistamine tablet.
templates.protocol_notes: Mild allergic reaction, no anaphylaxis features. BLS for transport and observation. ED for IV antihistamine and steroid. Monitor for progression.

templates.call_description: 1-year-old infant, fever 40°C, refusing feeds 2 days, now a purple non-blanching rash appearing on trunk and limbs. GCS 12. HR 168, BP 70/40, RR 58.
templates.protocol_notes: Paediatric meningococcal septicaemia — non-blanching purpuric rash = EMERGENCY. MICU — benzylpenicillin 300mg IV/IM immediately. Paediatric ICU. DIC management. Every minute matters.

templates.call_description: Horse kick to abdomen, farmer, 40 years. Severe pain left upper quadrant, guarding. Alert and talking. HR 112, BP 98/68. Last meal 1 hour ago.
templates.protocol_notes: High-energy blunt abdominal trauma. Splenic or hollow viscus injury likely. ALS, IV access, nil by mouth, trauma surgical hospital.

templates.call_description: 55-year-old male, acute alcohol withdrawal, last drink 48 hours ago. Grand mal seizure witnessed by family. Now post-ictal. Temperature 38.5C. HR 128, BP 180/110.
templates.protocol_notes: Alcohol withdrawal seizure — CIWA protocol. ALS for IV diazepam/lorazepam, thiamine 100mg IV before glucose. Risk of status epilepticus and delirium tremens. ICU capable ED.

templates.call_description: TB patient on DOTS, now in severe distress. Massive haemoptysis — coughing over 300mL blood in last 30 minutes. SpO2 82%. HR 130, BP 85/55. Pale.
templates.protocol_notes: Massive haemoptysis — airway flooding risk. MICU, position on affected side down, rigid bronchoscopy / balloon tamponade at hospital. Cardiothoracic surgery centre. Infection control.

templates.call_description: Elderly woman, 82, dizzy and fell, sitting on floor crying. No LOC, small haematoma on forearm, hip not shortened. Anticoagulated (warfarin). Alert, GCS 15, HR 78, BP 145/90.
templates.protocol_notes: Elderly fall on anticoagulation — higher bleeding risk. BLS, fall assessment, CT head if any neurology. Check INR. ED for fracture rule-out.

templates.call_description: 52-year-old male, post-CABG 3 years ago, now crushing left-sided chest pain, diaphoretic. Nitrate spray — no relief. BP 95/60, HR 55 (may be on BB). Wife says colour is off.
templates.protocol_notes: STEMI in post-CABG patient — graft thrombosis likely. MICU. Cath lab for emergency PCI of native vessel or graft. Higher procedural complexity.

templates.call_description: River Godavari flooding, Nashik district. Rescue team recovered 3 victims from swift current. One unresponsive, two alert but hypothermic. Rescue team asking for immediate ambulance support.
templates.protocol_notes: Multiple drowning victims — triage on site. ALS for critical victim. BLS for ambulatory. NDRF coordination. Hypothermia management — passive rewarming during transport.

templates.call_description: Mass zinc phosphide poisoning — farm labourers ate food stored near rat poison, Vidarbha. 8 affected. Progressive nausea, vomiting, garlic smell on breath, falling BP. Worst case HR 45, BP 60/30.
templates.protocol_notes: Zinc phosphide/phosphine mass poisoning — NO antidote. Supportive care. PPE for crew. Evacuate bystanders. Charcoal NOT effective. Toxicology ICU, magnesium sulphate for arrhythmia.

templates.call_description: Lightning strike during monsoon cricket match. 16-year-old, hit directly. Cardiac arrest, CPR started by coach. Witness says flash and bang then collapse. No pulse.
templates.protocol_notes: Lightning cardiac arrest — excellent prognosis with immediate CPR. Unlike other trauma arrests, aggressive resuscitation indicated. ALS, AED, any hospital ED. Check for secondary trauma from fall.

templates.call_description: ST bus vs bullock cart on single-lane highway, Marathwada. Bus overturned, 22 passengers, 3 bullock cart passengers. Reports of 2 deaths, 8 critical. Nearest hospital is 40km away.
templates.protocol_notes: Remote MCI in Marathwada — hospital far. Multi-unit response essential. START triage at scene. Request air ambulance. Mutual aid from adjacent districts. Police for road corridor. Nearest trauma centre within golden hour.

templates.call_description: 30-year-old construction worker, open fracture both bones right leg after scaffolding fall. Bone visible. Contaminated with soil. BP 110/75, HR 100. Alert.
templates.protocol_notes: Open fracture with contamination. ALS — sterile dressing, splint, IV antibiotics (cefazolin), IV analgesia. Tetanus prophylaxis. Orthopaedic trauma centre within 6 hours for washout.

templates.call_description: Hospital campus, nurse administered penicillin injection. Patient suddenly went limp. BP 50/20. Not breathing effectively. HR 140 then 40. Skin hives. CT room with crash cart nearby.
templates.protocol_notes: In-hospital anaphylaxis — code blue response. ALS in-hospital. IM adrenaline 0.5mg immediately. Supine, legs elevated. IV fluids rapid bolus. This requires in-hospital rapid response, not ambulance.

templates.call_description: Swimming pool accident — dived in shallow end. 20-year-old, neck pain, weakness all four limbs. Alert and talking. Floating in pool, bystanders holding head. HR 65, BP 85/55.
templates.protocol_notes: C-spine diving injury with neurogenic shock — log-roll extrication, cervical collar, spinal board. MICU for vasopressors. Neurosurgical centre with MRI. High cord injury may cause respiratory failure.

templates.call_description: Student hostel — 6 students with nausea, vomiting, visual problems (blurred, double vision), difficulty swallowing, descending weakness after eating canned food. Botulism pattern.
templates.protocol_notes: Foodborne botulism outbreak — descending paralysis pattern. Multiple ALS units. Antitoxin from state health department. ICU with ventilators — respiratory failure is leading cause of death. Alert food safety authority.

templates.call_description: Midwife called — placenta delivered but uterus not contracting. Post-partum haemorrhage. Pad soaked every 2 minutes. 30-year-old, third delivery, pale and anxious. HR 126, BP 80/55.
templates.protocol_notes: PPH with haemorrhagic shock. ALS — IV oxytocin 10 units IM + IV fluid wide open. Uterine massage. Bimanual compression if needed. O-negative blood. Obstetric surgical hospital — immediate theatre.

templates.call_description: Motorcyclist vs dumper truck. Ejected 15m. GCS 7, pupils asymmetric. Severe head injury suspected. Stabilised by bystanders. HR 56, BP 160/90 (Cushing's triad developing). RR 8 irregular.
templates.protocol_notes: Severe TBI with Cushing's response — impending herniation. MICU for RSI/intubation, target CO2 35mmHg (no hyperventilation). Mannitol 1g/kg. Neurosurgical centre with emergency theatre. Pupils asymmetric = blown pupil = craniectomy.

templates.call_description: Polling booth, peak summer election. 73-year-old queued for 3 hours. Collapsed after fainting, now unresponsive. Skin burning, dry. Estimated temperature 40.9°C. Crowd of 50 watching.
templates.protocol_notes: Heat stroke with loss of consciousness. ALS — aggressive cooling immediately, ice packs, wet sheets with fanning, cold IV fluids. Police for crowd control. Target <39°C before arrival.

templates.call_description: 34-year-old tall thin male, Marfan's syndrome known, suddenly severe central chest pain and feeling of doom. Examination by bystander doctor: BP difference between arms, new aortic regurgitation murmur.
templates.protocol_notes: Marfan's with aortic dissection Type A — surgical emergency. MICU. IV esmolol for rate and BP control. No tPA. Cardiothoracic surgical centre immediately.

templates.call_description: Neonatal jaundice — 5-day-old, yellow skin up to abdomen, very sleepy, not feeding. Mother worried since yesterday. Parents first-time. Baby born at district hospital, discharged day 2.
templates.protocol_notes: Neonatal jaundice with kernicterus risk — lethargy = urgent. BLS. Paediatric ED with bilirubin testing and phototherapy. Neonatology available.

templates.call_description: 25-year-old, road accident. Swollen deformed left wrist, obvious Colles fracture deformity. No neurovascular compromise. Alert, normal vitals. Moderate pain.
templates.protocol_notes: Colles fracture — isolated distal radius. BLS, splint, analgesia. ED for X-ray and manipulation/casting. Lowest priority.

templates.call_description: 50-year-old male farmer, leg wound from barbed wire 1 week ago, not healing. Wound now black and bubbling, foul smell. Fever 39.5C, HR 118, BP 98/68. Leg pain out of proportion.
templates.protocol_notes: Necrotising fasciitis — flesh-eating bacteria. Pain out of proportion + skin necrosis = surgical emergency. ALS, broad-spectrum antibiotics IV, IMMEDIATE surgical debridement. 1 hour = significant mortality increase.

templates.call_description: 65-year-old female, sudden palpitations 30 minutes. HR irregular at 160. No haemodynamic compromise. BP 130/85, no chest pain, slight breathlessness. History: hypertension.
templates.protocol_notes: New onset AF — if <48h can cardiovert. ALS for cardiac monitoring, IV metoprolol for rate control, anticoagulation consideration. Hospital with cardiology and echo.

templates.call_description: 8-year-old boy, stung by jellyfish while swimming at Juhu beach. Local pain, wheal at sting site on leg. Alert and crying. HR 96, BP 100/65. No systemic symptoms.
templates.protocol_notes: Minor jellyfish sting. BLS. Remove tentacles with gloved hand or card (not fingers). Seawater rinse (NOT fresh water). Hot water immersion for pain. ED observation for anaphylaxis signs.

templates.call_description: 40-year-old female with known kidney stone, sudden severe right flank pain, 10/10, radiating to groin, vomiting, cannot stay still. BP 150/95, HR 105. Afebrile.
templates.protocol_notes: Renal colic. BLS with analgesia (ketorolac IM). ED for CT KUB and urology. If febrile would be P1 (infected obstructed kidney).

templates.call_description: Nagpur coal mine collapse. 8 workers trapped underground. Methane gas detected. No injuries yet reported but access blocked. Rescue team on way. Medical standby required.
templates.protocol_notes: Mine rescue standby. DO NOT enter without gas clearance. MICU + ALS on standby at perimeter. Fire for gas monitoring. Police for perimeter. Crush syndrome protocol ready for extraction.

templates.call_description: Train derailment, Bhusawal junction. 3 coaches off rails. 40+ casualties reported. Station master calling for all available units. Structural hazards. Unknown number trapped.
templates.protocol_notes: Major MCI — train derailment. Declare state-level mass casualty. Multiple units from Nashik, Jalgaon, Aurangabad. Mutual aid. START triage. Level 1 trauma centres in multiple cities.

templates.call_description: School tiffin sharing — 15 children ill after lunch with vomiting and abdominal cramps. 3 with diarrhoea and fever. One child unconscious, convulsing. Food poisoning or deliberate contamination unknown.
templates.protocol_notes: Mass paediatric food poisoning. ALS for convulsing child (priority). BLS for moderate cases. Police for investigation and food sample preservation. Paediatric emergency centre.

templates.call_description: Air traffic controller, 44, suddenly pale and sweaty during high-stress shift. Mild central chest discomfort 3/10. Reluctant to leave post. Colleague insisting on calling 108. HR 88, BP 140/95.
templates.protocol_notes: Stress-precipitated STEMI — underreported symptoms common in professionals. MICU. Relieve from duty. Cath lab activation. Do not delay.

templates.call_description: 52-year-old male, sudden onset facial droop, arm weakness with concurrent severe breathlessness and pleuritic right chest pain. FAST positive. SpO2 84%. HR 115 irregular.
templates.protocol_notes: Concurrent stroke AND pulmonary embolism — possible paradoxical embolus (PFO). MICU. Competing treatment (thrombolysis for PE vs contraindicated for stroke). Stroke unit with cardiac capability. Complex case.

templates.call_description: 60-year-old office worker, chest pain when breathing deeply after coughing fit. No injury. Normal vitals, mild discomfort 3/10. Alert. History of osteoporosis.
templates.protocol_notes: Likely rib fracture from cough — osteoporosis patient. BLS, analgesia, X-ray at ED. Low priority unless respiratory compromise.

templates.call_description: 17-year-old girl, found collapsed at school. Teacher says she has been hiding a pregnancy. Waters apparently broken. Fully dilated, head visible. School has no medical facility.
templates.protocol_notes: Precipitate delivery — imminent birth at school. ALS capable of delivery. Prepare resuscitaire for neonate. Adolescent safeguarding protocols. Obstetric hospital with NICU.

templates.call_description: 28-year-old returned from Maldives 5 days ago, now high fever 40.2C, rigors, headache, vomiting. Peripheral blood film taken by family physician shows ring forms. BP 88/58, HR 128. Jaundice developing.
templates.protocol_notes: Severe falciparum malaria — tropical disease emergency. ALS, IV artesunate (first line), strict fluid management. Infectious disease hospital. Malaria with organ failure is life-threatening.

templates.call_description: Expressway tanker accident — petroleum tanker rolled. Driver trapped. Fuel spilling. No ignition yet. 2 other vehicle occupants injured nearby. Traffic backed up 2km.
templates.protocol_notes: Hazmat with entrapment. Fire for fuel hazard control and extrication. EMS at safe standby distance until cleared. No sparks near scene. MICU for driver. Police for traffic corridor. Level 1 trauma.

templates.call_description: 65-year-old male, Alzheimer's disease, wandered from home, found confused in traffic. No injury. Unable to state address. Agitated when approached. Family searching for him.
templates.protocol_notes: Dementia patient found wandering. BLS for safe transport. Police for identity verification and family contact. Mental health assessment at ED. No physical emergency.

templates.call_description: 16-year-old male, right lower quadrant pain for 12 hours, worse with movement. Nausea, vomiting twice, no appetite. Rebound tenderness on right. Low-grade fever 37.9C. HR 96, BP 115/78.
templates.protocol_notes: Appendicitis — Alvarado score likely high. BLS, nil by mouth, analgesia. Surgical hospital for USS/CT and appendicectomy. Not immediately life-threatening but within 6-12h of perforation risk.

templates.call_description: Auto-rickshaw minor collision, 3 occupants. All ambulatory, no visible injuries, one with whiplash pain. Alert, GCS 15 all three. No LOC. Moderate speed impact.
templates.protocol_notes: Minor RTA, ambulatory victims. BLS — single unit can transport all 3. Police for accident report. Soft collar for whiplash. ED for clearance.

templates.call_description: 22-year-old with cystic fibrosis, massive haemoptysis 200mL, SpO2 88%, pale. Known bronchiectasis. Current admission planned for tomorrow. Now acute deterioration. HR 122.
templates.protocol_notes: CF with massive haemoptysis. ALS — position bleeding side down, O2, IV tranexamic acid, bronchoscopy at hospital. Cardiothoracic surgery capability. CF specialist centre preferred.

templates.call_description: 20-year-old competitive swimmer, training pool. Cardiac arrest in pool. Lifeguard pulled out, CPR started. AED delivered 2 shocks. Not regained consciousness. Bystanders doing effective CPR.
templates.protocol_notes: Young athlete cardiac arrest — consider long QT, HOCM, Brugada, commotio cordis. MICU for advanced resuscitation, targeted temperature management post-ROSC. Cath lab + cardiac genetics centre.

templates.call_description: 45-year-old farmer, accidental gunshot wound to right shoulder from neighbour's rifle while cleaning. Bleeding controlled by pressure. Entry only, no exit wound. Alert, BP 118/80, HR 88.
templates.protocol_notes: Gunshot wound — Police mandatory. ALS for pressure dressing, IV access, analgesia. Do NOT remove bullet. Vascular injury assessment. Trauma surgical hospital for exploration.

templates.call_description: Newborn, 12 hours old, cyanosis central, worsening despite oxygen. Born at home. Loud murmur. Ductus-dependent lesion suspected by phone-guidance doctor. HR 180, RR 66.
templates.protocol_notes: Ductus-dependent congenital heart disease — prostaglandin E1 IV to keep duct open. MICU for neonatal resuscitation capability. Paediatric cardiac surgical centre. Keep SpO2 75-85% — higher O2 closes duct.

templates.call_description: 35-year-old leptospirosis patient (confirmed), deteriorating. Jaundice, oliguria for 18 hours, petechiae, coughing blood. BP 70/40, HR 138. Weil's disease.
templates.protocol_notes: Weil's disease (severe leptospirosis) — multi-organ failure. MICU. Penicillin IV, dialysis for renal failure, ventilation if pulmonary haemorrhage. Tertiary centre with nephrology + ICU.

templates.call_description: RTA victim with ejection. 28-year-old, no seatbelt. GCS 13, cervical tenderness, ambulatory at scene but unsteady. Brachial plexus injury suspected. HR 95, BP 120/80.
templates.protocol_notes: C-spine and brachial plexus mechanism. ALS with full spinal precautions. GCS 13 = head injury. CT spine + head. Neurosurgical hospital.

templates.call_description: Farmer, electrocuted by downed powerline post-cyclone. Found 50m from line. Not breathing. No pulse. Bystanders unsure if power is off. Scene potentially live.
templates.protocol_notes: Electrocution cardiac arrest — DO NOT approach until Fire confirms power off. ALS for CPR and defibrillation. Excellent prognosis if immediate resuscitation. Burns, rhabdomyolysis, spinal injury all possible.

templates.call_description: 45-year-old male, alcoholic, known ascites. Acute abdominal pain, high fever, confusion. Abdomen rigid and tender diffusely. HR 128, BP 80/50. GCS 12.
templates.protocol_notes: Spontaneous bacterial peritonitis in liver failure patient. ALS, IV cefotaxime 2g, aggressive fluid with human albumin. Hepatology + ICU hospital.

templates.call_description: Patient with motor neuron disease (ALS), using home BiPAP. Machine alarming, patient in respiratory distress, CO2 retention symptoms: flushed, drowsy, headache. RR 10, SpO2 89%.
templates.protocol_notes: MND acute respiratory failure — BiPAP failure. MICU with ventilator. Advance care planning discussion if available. Neurology + ICU hospital. Complex ethical decisions may be needed.

templates.call_description: 2-year-old fell into household bucket of water. Mother pulled out after estimated 20 seconds. Baby crying loudly, pink, alert, no respiratory distress. Normal vitals.
templates.protocol_notes: Near drowning, very brief submersion, asymptomatic. BLS — observe for 4-6 hours for delayed pulmonary oedema. Paediatric ED.

templates.call_description: Security guard reports suspicious liquid in envelopes received at corporate office, Pune. 2 employees touching envelopes feel skin tingling and chest tightness. White powder visible. Bioterrorism protocol suspected.
templates.protocol_notes: Suspected CBRN incident. DO NOT enter without full HAZMAT protection. Evacuate building. PPE Level A until agent identified. Fire for decontamination. ALS in protective gear. Alert NDMA.

templates.call_description: Mass motorcycle accident during procession. 12 bikes piled up. 6 riders unconscious, 4 with apparent fractures, 2 walking and talking. Mix of helmeted and unhelmeted.
templates.protocol_notes: Multi-motorcycle MCI. START triage immediately. MICU for unconscious, ALS for fractures, BLS for walking wounded. Multiple Level 1 and secondary hospitals needed.

templates.call_description: 15-year-old with newly diagnosed diabetes, not eating for 2 days. Now deep rapid breathing (Kussmaul), fruity breath, drowsy. Blood glucose 28 mmol/L. HR 112, BP 98/65.
templates.protocol_notes: DKA in teenager. ALS — saline 10mL/kg bolus (cautious in paediatric DKA — cerebral oedema risk). Insulin only after fluid correction. Paediatric ED with endocrine input.

templates.call_description: 55-year-old female smoker, progressive breathlessness over 3 days, now resting tachypnoea RR 32, accessory muscle use. New bilateral infiltrates on phone-shared X-ray. Temperature 38.9C. SpO2 88%.
templates.protocol_notes: ARDS — high-flow O2, prone position consideration, non-invasive ventilation attempt before intubation. ICU with ventilator mandatory. Any cause investigation at hospital.

templates.call_description: Holi festival. 22-year-old male, colour thrown in eyes, painful red eyes, tearing, photophobia. Vision blurred. No other injuries. Alert.
templates.protocol_notes: Chemical eye injury — IRRIGATE eyes immediately with saline or water (litres). BLS. Ophthalmology ED. Remove contact lenses if present.

templates.call_description: 14-year-old, dengue fever confirmed day 5. Now severe abdominal pain, restless, plasma leakage signs. Platelet reportedly 8000. BP dropping 90/60. Haematocrit rising.
templates.protocol_notes: Severe dengue with plasma leakage and impending shock. ALS — cautious IV fluid (dengue protocol, NOT aggressive). Platelet transfusion if <10000 or bleeding. Paediatric ED with haematology.

templates.call_description: 50-year-old welder, flash burn to face and both hands. Both eyes shut, can't see. 12% BSA superficial burns. Alert. No respiratory symptoms. HR 96, BP 125/82.
templates.protocol_notes: Flash burn with ocular involvement. ALS — copious eye irrigation, cool dressings (NOT ice). Burns unit for skin + ophthalmology for eye injury. Urgent. No inhalation injury.

templates.call_description: 14-year-old female, severe lower abdominal pain, nausea, right adnexal mass on clinical exam by school nurse. Temp normal. Sudden onset 30 minutes ago. HR 108, BP 105/72.
templates.protocol_notes: Ovarian torsion — time-critical for ovarian salvage. ALS, analgesia. Gynaecological surgical hospital within 6 hours for viability. USS on arrival.

templates.call_description: 35-year-old male, sudden VT on gym heart rate monitor, currently unresponsive, bystander starting CPR. AED brought from reception. No previous cardiac history known.
templates.protocol_notes: VT cardiac arrest in young adult — AED + CPR. MICU. Post-ROSC: cath lab, electrophysiology study. Consider channelopathy, HOCM, coronary anomaly. Defibrillator implant discussion.

templates.call_description: Bus fall, Nashik ghat. 55-year-old woman, multiple rib fractures right side (clinical), reduced breath sounds. SpO2 92%. HR 105, BP 105/75. Alert. Crepitus on palpation right chest.
templates.protocol_notes: Multiple rib fractures with possible haemothorax. ALS — O2, IV access, pain control critical (splinting prevents breathing). Monitor for tension PTX. Surgical trauma hospital.

templates.call_description: 85-year-old female, terminal cancer, expected to die at palliative care home. Found unresponsive, pulseless. No DNR in their system. Palliative team calling for guidance. Family wants 'everything done'.
templates.protocol_notes: Expected palliative death. BLS for confirmation and family support. 1 cycle CPR for legal clarity given no DNR. Futile resuscitation avoidance — escalate to palliative physician for DNAR guidance.

templates.call_description: 55-year-old, cut finger chopping vegetables. Deep laceration distal phalanx with partial flexor tendon visible. Bleeding controlled. Alert, stable vitals.
templates.protocol_notes: Tendon injury — needs plastic/orthopaedic surgery for repair. BLS. Keep clean dressing, elevated. Plastic surgery ED. Not immediate life-threat.

templates.call_description: Pesticide company worker, accidental topical exposure to paraquat while loading trucks, gloves not worn. No ingestion. Redness on hands and arms. Feels fine now but colleagues are panicked.
templates.protocol_notes: Paraquat skin exposure — decontaminate immediately (strip, wash with water + soap 15 min). ALS. Even skin exposure can cause delayed multi-organ failure. Toxicology hospital essential. No antidote — supportive care.

templates.call_description: 5-year-old boy, febrile, develops sudden limp, refusing to walk. Painful internal rotation of left hip. Temperature 38.6C. No obvious skin lesion. HR 104.
templates.protocol_notes: Septic arthritis vs transient synovitis — Kocher criteria needed. BLS for transport. Orthopaedic paediatric centre for urgent washout if septic arthritis. Time-sensitive for joint preservation.

templates.call_description: 58-year-old with sickle cell disease, sudden onset right-sided weakness and aphasia. Known to have had multiple TIAs. GCS 14. BP 140/90. FAST strongly positive.
templates.protocol_notes: Stroke in sickle cell — exchange transfusion preferred over thrombolysis. Hydrate en route, avoid hypoxia. Haematology + stroke centre. Hydroxyurea continuation.

templates.call_description: Agricultural accident. Tractor rollover on field, 55-year-old male, partially trapped under tractor wheel (now lifted by workers). Alert, hip and pelvis pain, cannot move legs. BP 88/55, HR 130.
templates.protocol_notes: Tractor crush with unstable pelvis + haemorrhagic shock. MICU — pelvic binder immediately, massive transfusion protocol, permissive hypotension. Level 1 surgical trauma centre for angioembolisation or OR.

templates.call_description: 23-year-old, G3P2 (previous 2 caesareans), 36 weeks, sudden onset severe upper abdominal pain and uterine pain with vaginal bleeding. BP 140/90. Uterus hard and tender. HR 120.
templates.protocol_notes: Uterine rupture risk in previous LSCS scar — catastrophic. ALS. IV access, O-negative blood, immediate obstetric surgical hospital. Neonatal team on standby. Minutes count.

templates.call_description: 4-year-old, bilious vomiting since morning, distended abdomen, not passed stool in 3 days. Occasional blood in nappy. Crying inconsolably, then suddenly quiet (sausage-shaped mass felt).
templates.protocol_notes: Intussusception — currant jelly stool + sausage mass + intermittent screaming = classic triad. ALS. Paediatric ED for air enema reduction or surgical reduction. Time critical.

templates.call_description: 26-year-old female marathon runner, collapsed at finish line, 10m before. CPR started by race doctor. AED shows shockable rhythm. 4 shocks delivered. ROSC briefly but lost. Continued CPR.
templates.protocol_notes: Refractory VF — consider ECMO (eCPR). MICU for advanced resuscitation. Cath lab for emergent coronary angiography post-ROSC. Target temperature management. High sodium bicarbonate dosing for prolonged arrest.

templates.call_description: 62-year-old on warfarin, large bruise and painful swelling right forearm after minor bump on table. No deformity, neurovascular intact. Alert, normal vitals. INR reportedly 4.8 last week.
templates.protocol_notes: Supra-therapeutic anticoagulation with trauma. BLS — check INR, vitamin K consideration. Head CT only if any head injury. ED for haematoma assessment and anticoagulation reversal discussion.

templates.call_description: Earthquake M5.2, Osmanabad district. Multiple calls simultaneously — building collapses reported. 3 dead confirmed. 15+ trapped. NDRF alerted. State requesting all available medical resources.
templates.protocol_notes: Earthquake MCI — Osmanabad seismic zone. State surge declaration. All Marathwada resources. NDRF for search and rescue. Crush syndrome protocol for extraction. Multiple hospitals in Aurangabad and Latur.

templates.call_description: 40-year-old male, known myasthenia gravis, on pyridostigmine and steroids. Sudden worsening dyspnoea, cannot lift head from pillow, voice nasal. Swallowing difficult. SpO2 91%. HR 88.
templates.protocol_notes: Myasthenic crisis — impending respiratory failure. MICU for ventilation. Do NOT give succinylcholine. IVIG or plasmapheresis at hospital. Neurology + ICU. Avoid aminoglycosides and beta-blockers.

templates.call_description: Slum fire, Dharavi sector. 35-year-old male, runs out of burning structure. Burns face and both arms, 20% BSA superficial to partial thickness. No respiratory symptoms currently. HR 108, BP 120/80.
templates.protocol_notes: 20% BSA burns — ALS for IV Parkland formula fluids, analgesia, burn dressings. Monitor airway — smoke exposure in enclosed space raises inhalation injury risk even without symptoms. Burns unit.

templates.call_description: 6-year-old, acute epiglottitis. Sitting up, drooling, stridor, muffled voice, very high fever 40.5°C. Looks scared and toxic. Parents say onset 4 hours ago and rapidly worsening.
templates.protocol_notes: Acute epiglottitis — DO NOT examine throat (can precipitate complete obstruction). ALS — do not distress child, upright position, blow-by oxygen only. Call ahead to paediatric anaesthesia for controlled airway in theatre.

templates.call_description: 38-year-old female, acute onset left shoulder and arm pain after gym session. Feels dizzy. HR 92, BP 130/85. Colleague is a paramedic and says 12-lead shows STEMI in inferior leads.
templates.protocol_notes: Inferior STEMI in young female — often atypical presentation (shoulder/arm only). MICU, aspirin 325mg, heparin as per protocol. RV infarction screen — avoid nitrates if RV involved.

templates.call_description: Industrial press accident. 35-year-old, right hand partially degloving and crush injury. Factory first-aider has tourniquet applied. Alert, HR 105, BP 112/78. Pale and shaking.
templates.protocol_notes: Degloving + crush — plastic surgery for replantation/reconstruction. ALS for tourniquet verification, IV access, analgesia. Preserve degloved skin in moist sterile dressings. Replantation centre within 6 hours.

templates.call_description: 70-year-old immunocompromised (chemotherapy last week), fever 38.8°C, no localising source. Severely neutropenic per last blood test. HR 108, BP 105/72. Looking unwell. Temp 38.8C.
templates.protocol_notes: Neutropenic sepsis — oncological emergency. ALS — IV broad spectrum antibiotics within 60 minutes (piperacillin-tazobactam). Reverse barrier nursing. Oncology-capable hospital with haematology.

templates.call_description: 26-year-old gym user, heard pop in right knee during squat. Knee swollen rapidly (haemarthrosis). Cannot straighten. Walking extremely difficult. Stable vitals.
templates.protocol_notes: ACL rupture with haemarthrosis — not immediately life-threatening. BLS, RICE, knee splint. Orthopaedic ED for MRI and surgical planning. Elective not emergency.

templates.call_description: 55-year-old male, sudden severe mid-abdominal and back pain, pain radiating to both sides. History of AAA on surveillance. BP 85/50, HR 138. Diaphoretic, pale. Pulsatile abdominal mass.
templates.protocol_notes: Ruptured AAA — same as T084. MICU, permissive hypotension. Vascular surgical hospital. OR waiting.

templates.call_description: Three people at Kolhapur MIDC area experiencing skin and eye irritation, breathlessness, strong bleach smell. Nearby factory reported chlorine gas release. 15+ people evacuating.
templates.protocol_notes: Industrial chlorine gas MCI. Multi-ALS response. Fire for HAZMAT containment. Police for evacuation zone. Decon before hospital transport. Bronchodilators, steroid nebulisers.

templates.call_description: 2-month-old infant, brought by grandmother — not waking, limp, cold peripheries, very pale. No known history. Parents not reachable. Grandmother found baby this way after nap.
templates.protocol_notes: SIDS/ALTE (apparent life-threatening event) — ALS for paediatric resuscitation. Police mandatory — safeguarding and SIDS investigation protocol. Paediatric ICU. Social services.

templates.call_description: Elderly man, 78, on anticoagulants, minor head knock from slipping. No LOC. Now headache 6/10 worsening over 90 minutes. GCS 14. BP 165/95. Neurologically intact still.
templates.protocol_notes: Expanding subdural haematoma in anticoagulated elderly — classic delayed presentation. ALS, reverse anticoagulation (vitamin K + PCC/FFP). Neurosurgical hospital for CT and possible drainage. Lucid interval ending.

templates.call_description: 30-year-old post-thyroidectomy day 2, sudden muscle cramps, tingling around mouth and hands, facial spasm when tapping cheek (Chvostek positive). QTc prolonged on ECG at clinic.
templates.protocol_notes: Post-thyroidectomy hypocalcaemia. ALS — IV calcium gluconate 10mL over 10 min. Cardiac monitoring (QTc prolongation → VT risk). Endocrine hospital. Magnesium check.

templates.call_description: School bus accident on Malshej Ghat road. Bus partially over cliff edge, 40 children on board. Engine running. Unstable position. 6 windows broken, screaming heard. No deaths reported yet.
templates.protocol_notes: Paediatric MCI on ghat — extreme scenario. Fire stabilise vehicle before entry. Police for road closure. ALL available paediatric ALS and MICU from Nashik and Pune. Multiple paediatric ED hospitals.

templates.call_description: 55-year-old with epistaxis — nosebleed for 40 minutes that won't stop. Hypertensive on amlodipine. BP 195/105. Alert. No other complaints. Leaning forward, blood dripping.
templates.protocol_notes: Posterior epistaxis — pinch anterior nares, lean forward. BLS. ED for nasal packing, BP management. Blood pressure likely contributing — antihypertensive review.

templates.call_description: Gas cylinder explosion in chawl building. 3 floors partially collapsed. 20+ residents trapped. Fire and debris. Several on fire. Other residents assisting.
templates.protocol_notes: MCI blast + burns + structural collapse. State-level MCI. Multiple MICU (blast and burns), ALS and BLS. Fire for structural safety and fire suppression. Police for perimeter and crowd. Multiple hospitals: burns units + level 1 trauma.

templates.call_description: Community — 8 people from same wedding developed high fever 40°C, profuse diarrhoea, some with blood in stool, 48 hours after event. 2 elderly now confused. Typhoid or Shigella outbreak suspected.
templates.protocol_notes: Outbreak — ALS for critical elderly, BLS for stable. Blood cultures, stool cultures, IV antibiotics (ceftriaxone). Notify IDSP immediately. Food samples from caterer. Multiple hospitals.

templates.call_description: 13-year-old, Pune, found unresponsive by parents who returned home. Empty tablet strips nearby (parental diazepam). Breathing slowly. Pinpoint pupils partially. GCS 7. Pulse 80. BP 100/65.
templates.protocol_notes: Paediatric benzodiazepine overdose with suicidal intent. ALS — airway, flumazenil only if resuscitation context (short action), activated charcoal if <1h and protected airway. Paediatric ED + psychiatric liaison. Police for safeguarding.

templates.call_description: 28-year-old cyclist, no helmet, hit by car. GCS 12, blood from ear (haemotympanum). Raccoon eyes developing. Battle's sign starting. BP 140/90, HR 78.
templates.protocol_notes: Basal skull fracture — haemotympanum, raccoon eyes, Battle's sign. GCS 12 = significant head injury. ALS with full C-spine precautions. Neurosurgical centre with CT. No nasal tube insertion.

templates.call_description: 42-year-old with bilateral ureteric obstruction from cancer, now urosepsis — shaking rigors, fever 40.5°C, back pain, HR 128, BP 78/48, drowsy.
templates.protocol_notes: Obstructive urosepsis — infected obstruction = life threatening. ALS, IV antibiotics (piperacillin-taz), aggressive fluids. Urology + ICU hospital for emergency nephrostomy/stenting. Sepsis bundle.

templates.call_description: Pilgrimage crowd, Shirdi. Elderly 80-year-old male in a crowd crush, not breathing, found after crowd dispersed. Unknown downtime. No CPR happening. Hot and humid conditions.
templates.protocol_notes: Probable crowd crush cardiac arrest with unknown prolonged downtime in heat. Expectant in MCI context — limited resuscitation resources for salvageable patients. BLS confirm, Police body management.

templates.call_description: Factory accident — lathe machine caught worker's hand. 38-year-old, degloving and crush of 3 fingers on dominant hand. Alert. Tourniquet applied. BP 120/80, HR 94.
templates.protocol_notes: Digital degloving/amputation — replantation window 6h. ALS. Preserve digits in cold moist sterile wrapping. Plastic/microvascular surgical centre. Viable digits have good replantation outcomes.

templates.call_description: 30-year-old male, scuba diver, ascended too rapidly. Now right arm weakness, confusion, joint pain, and shortness of breath 40 minutes after surfacing. SpO2 91%. HR 108. BP 130/85. Buddy diver on scene.
templates.protocol_notes: Type II decompression sickness — neurological and pulmonary involvement. ALS, 100% O2 via non-rebreather immediately (accelerates nitrogen washout), IV fluids. ONLY definitive treatment is hyperbaric oxygen chamber. Transport to HBO facility — nearest is INS Hamla Mumbai or KEM.

templates.call_description: 45-year-old hypertensive male, sudden onset bilateral visual loss — total blackout both eyes for 3 minutes then partial recovery. Atrial fibrillation known. BP 190/120. Currently seeing only half of visual field.
templates.protocol_notes: Posterior circulation stroke — basilar artery territory. Visual field deficit + AF = cardioembolic. ALS, stroke unit with MRI and posterior fossa capability. Thrombolysis or mechanical thrombectomy decision.

templates.call_description: 48-year-old male, drowning in flooded basement during heavy monsoon. Coworkers pulled him out after 4-minute submersion. CPR started. Cold water exposure. AED not available. No pulse.
templates.protocol_notes: Cold water drowning arrest — hypothermia protective. Continue CPR until warm and dead. ALS, rewarm during transport. Fire for scene safety (structural flooding). ICU with active rewarming capability.

templates.call_description: 11-year-old girl, sickle cell disease crisis, excruciating bone pain in legs, arms, and chest for past 6 hours, not responding to oral analgesia. Temperature 38.1C. SpO2 93%. Crying continuously.
templates.protocol_notes: Acute chest syndrome / vaso-occlusive crisis in sickle cell. ALS — high-flow O2, IV morphine titrated, IV fluids, temperature management. Haematology paediatric centre. Exchange transfusion if ACS confirmed.

templates.call_description: 22-year-old, road accident, left clavicle fracture, arm in sling applied by bystander. Alert, GCS 15, neurovascular intact distal. Mild pain 4/10. No other injuries. Stable vitals.
templates.protocol_notes: Isolated clavicle fracture — non-operative management. BLS, broad arm sling, analgesia. ED for X-ray and orthopaedic follow-up. Low acuity.

templates.call_description: 65-year-old immunocompromised (HIV, CD4 200), high fever 40.2C, productive cough with blood-tinged sputum, progressive dyspnoea over 5 days. Night sweats for 3 weeks. SpO2 85%. HR 118.
templates.protocol_notes: PCP (Pneumocystis pneumonia) in HIV — ALS. High flow O2. IV co-trimoxazole + steroids. Infection control precautions. HIV-capable infectious disease hospital with ICU.

templates.call_description: Pune pharmaceutical factory fire. Multiple storage drums of solvents ablaze. 12 workers trapped in warehouse. Toxic fumes reported. 3 workers already evacuated with burns and inhalation injuries.
templates.protocol_notes: Chemical industrial fire MCI. HAZMAT + inhalation injury + burns. Fire for containment and rescue. EMS full PPE decontamination zone. Multiple MICU + ALS. Burns unit + toxicology hospital.

templates.call_description: 22-year-old college student, 8 hours of severe epigastric pain after binge drinking, nausea, 4 vomiting episodes. Now fever 38.5C, HR 110, BP 100/72. Lipase reportedly very high by campus physician.
templates.protocol_notes: Alcohol-induced acute pancreatitis — Ranson's criteria suggest severe. ALS, aggressive IV crystalloid, nil by mouth, analgesia. ICU-capable surgical hospital. ERCP may be needed.

templates.call_description: 48-year-old male, industrial blast injury — works at fireworks factory, Sivakasi supplier. Face, hands, and chest affected. Both eyes injured, cannot see. 30% BSA flash burns. Alert. HR 112.
templates.protocol_notes: Fireworks blast and burns. ALS — copious eye irrigation, burn wound care, Parkland formula. Ophthalmology + burns unit. Investigate factory safety violation.

templates.call_description: 18-year-old student, history of epilepsy, found in classroom after witnessed generalised seizure lasting 3 minutes. Now post-ictal. Medic alert bracelet says 'Brugada syndrome'. HR 72, BP 110/70. GCS 12.
templates.protocol_notes: Brugada syndrome — seizure may represent VF with rapid ROSC. ALS with defibrillator ready. Avoid Na-channel blockers (no flecainide, avoid tricyclics). Cardiology with EP study capability. ICD discussion.

templates.call_description: Street drug emergency — 5 young adults at rave, all collapsed in sequence. Suspected MDMA (ecstasy) + alcohol. Two fitting, one hyperthermia 41°C with altered consciousness, two agitated and confused. HR 155.
templates.protocol_notes: MDMA toxidrome — serotonin syndrome + hyperthermia. ALS, aggressive cooling (target <39°C), IV benzodiazepines for seizures/agitation. Avoid cyproheptadine. Police for scene. Multiple units for 5 patients. ICU with toxicology.

templates.call_description: Road accident — elderly cyclist, 72, vs car. No helmet. Rib fractures right 4-8, haemopneumothorax suspected. SpO2 89%, decreased breath sounds right. Alert but deteriorating. HR 118, BP 95/68.
templates.protocol_notes: Massive haemothorax — ALS for needle decompression if tension component, chest seal. Blood type and cross-match en route. Cardiothoracic surgical hospital for chest drain and possible thoracotomy.

templates.call_description: 5-year-old child returned from Bangladesh visit, fever 40.8°C for 5 days, now drowsy, enlarged spleen felt by parent, yellow eyes. Blood smear done at local lab — thick ring forms. GCS 12.
templates.protocol_notes: Severe paediatric falciparum malaria with cerebral involvement. ALS — IV artesunate (paediatric dosing 3mg/kg), glucose monitoring (hypoglycaemia common), seizure management. Paediatric ICU with tropical disease capability.

templates.call_description: 19-year-old primigravida, 39 weeks, onset of generalised tonic-clonic seizure at home. No previous pregnancy issues. Now post-ictal. Family says she was complaining of severe headache and blurry vision yesterday.
templates.protocol_notes: Eclampsia — MICU for airway management, IV magnesium sulphate loading dose (4g IV over 5-10 min), antihypertensives. Emergency delivery indicated. Tertiary obstetric centre with HDU/ICU.

templates.call_description: Bomb blast at market area. Multiple casualties — caller reports 10+ injured, debris injuries, blast wave injuries. Structural fires. Secondary device risk cannot be excluded. Police on site.
templates.protocol_notes: Terrorist blast MCI — secondary device risk: do NOT rush in. Police EOD clearance mandatory first. Staged approach. MICU + ALS. Blast injury triad. State terrorism protocol. Multiple level 1 trauma centres.

templates.call_description: 52-year-old ESRD patient, on dialysis 3x/week, missed session yesterday. Found pulseless by dialysis nurse on arrival. ECG monitor shows sine wave pattern — severe hyperkalaemia. CPR started.
templates.protocol_notes: Hyperkalaemic cardiac arrest — sine wave = imminent standstill. MICU: IV calcium gluconate IMMEDIATELY (membrane protection), sodium bicarbonate, dextrose-insulin, continue CPR. Dialysis facility for emergent HD.

templates.call_description: 14-year-old sports injury — high-speed cricket ball to temple, no helmet worn. Dizzy, mild headache, 5 seconds LOC witnessed. Now alert GCS 15. No vomiting. Pupil equal and reactive.
templates.protocol_notes: Concussion with brief LOC. BLS — SCAT5 assessment, paediatric head injury CT criteria (PECARN). No return to sport. ED observation and CT if indicated.

templates.call_description: 65-year-old male with known large bowel cancer, sudden severe left-sided abdominal pain, distension, no flatus for 24 hours. Now vomiting faecal material. Temperature 38.8C. HR 116, BP 90/60.
templates.protocol_notes: Obstructed bowel with perforation signs. ALS, nil by mouth, IV antibiotics, fluid resuscitation. Surgical hospital for emergency colostomy/resection. Oncology team informed.

templates.call_description: 55-year-old male smoker, 3 weeks progressive dyspnoea and leg swelling. Now sitting bolt upright, frothy pink sputum, cannot speak. SpO2 78% on 4L O2. BP 195/110, HR 124, RR 42. Bilateral crackles.
templates.protocol_notes: Acute cardiogenic pulmonary oedema — MICU. CPAP/BiPAP immediately, GTN IV if SBP>110, frusemide IV 80mg. Upright position. If SBP<90 — inotropes, not vasodilators. Cardiology hospital.

templates.call_description: 7-year-old, accidental paracetamol overdose — grandparent gave double dose over 3 days thinking it was prescribed dose. Child jaundiced today, abdominal pain, lethargy. LFTs reportedly high.
templates.protocol_notes: Delayed paracetamol hepatotoxicity in child. ALS — N-acetylcysteine IV (Rumack-Matthew nomogram). Paediatric hepatology hospital. Transplant assessment may be needed. Jaundice day 3+ = serious.

templates.call_description: 30-year-old MMA fighter, match injury — multiple facial fractures confirmed by ringside physician, epistaxis difficult to control, GCS 14, left eye ptosis. Medical staff requesting hospital transfer.
templates.protocol_notes: Panfacial fractures + GCS 14 + ptosis suggesting orbital injury. ALS for airway monitoring (bleeding may compromise). Police mandatory for sporting event injury. Maxillofacial + neurosurgery centre.

templates.call_description: Landslide, Pune Ghats during heavy monsoon. Village of 200 partially buried. NDRF team dispatched. District collector declaring emergency. 30+ persons unaccounted. 8 confirmed rescued with injuries.
templates.protocol_notes: Landslide MCI — Pune district. All Pune + Nashik medical resources mobilised. NDRF for rescue. Crush syndrome protocol. Air evacuation from ghat if road cut. State surge declaration. Sassoon + IGGMC Nagpur as receiving hospitals.

templates.call_description: 45-year-old, syncope on train platform, bystander ECG shows 3rd degree heart block — P-waves and QRS completely dissociated. Currently conscious but dizzy. HR 34, BP 90/60.
templates.protocol_notes: Complete (3rd degree) heart block — unstable. MICU — transcutaneous pacing immediately, atropine unlikely to work (ventricular escape). Temporary transvenous pacing at cardiology centre. Permanent pacemaker urgently.

templates.call_description: 40-year-old office worker, acute disc prolapse, sudden severe back pain while lifting file box, radiating down right leg to foot (sciatica). Cannot stand up straight. Alert, vitals normal.
templates.protocol_notes: Acute disc prolapse — not an emergency unless cauda equina (bladder/bowel/bilateral symptoms). BLS for transport comfort. ED for analgesia and neurology referral. MRI as outpatient.

templates.call_description: Naval dockyard accident, Mumbai. Worker fell into dry dock — 14m fall onto concrete. GCS 5, bilateral pupil dilation, blood from ears, extensive head and torso injuries. HR 52, BP 195/105 (Cushing).
templates.protocol_notes: Catastrophic TBI with bilateral blown pupils — poor prognosis but not automatic expectant. MICU RSI, mannitol 1g/kg, target ICP management, neurosurgery for decompressive craniectomy decision. Naval medical clearance for access.

templates.call_description: Tribal village, Gadchiroli. 3-year-old with severe acute malnutrition, multiple infected skin lesions, fever 39.8°C, oedema, hair discoloured. Cannot walk. HR 140, RR 52. No proper healthcare for 2 months.
