templates.protocol_notes: SAM with sepsis in remote tribal area. BLS adequate for transport — no ALS resources nearby. F-75 therapeutic food protocol. Antibiotics per IMNCI guidelines. Paediatric hospital for ReSoMal fluids (not standard ORS — dangerous in SAM). Alert nutrition rehabilitation centre.

templates.call_description: 25-year-old marathon runner, post-race collapse. Myoglobin-dark brown urine visible in urinary catheter bag. No urine output for 2 hours. Severe muscle pain, swollen calves. Creatinine reportedly 8.4.
templates.protocol_notes: Exertional rhabdomyolysis with AKI. ALS — aggressive IV crystalloid (200mL/hr target to flush myoglobin), urinary alkalinisation (sodium bicarbonate in fluids). Dialysis centre for HD if oliguric. Treat hyperkalaemia.

templates.call_description: Child abuse case — 4-year-old brought by neighbour, multiple bruises in different stages of healing, 2 healing fractures of forearm (accidental explanation inconsistent), retinal haemorrhages suspected, shaken baby.
templates.protocol_notes: Non-accidental injury (child abuse) — do NOT return child to parents. ALS for assessment. Police mandatory. Child Protection Services. Paediatric ED with social work, ophthalmology for retinal haemorrhage, skeletal survey. CT head for subdural.

templates.call_description: 45-year-old woman, 80% BSA full-thickness burns from house fire. Found outside burning structure. Airway already compromised — stridor, no voice. Burns to face, neck, trunk, all extremities. HR 130, BP 90/60.
templates.protocol_notes: 80% BSA burns with airway compromise — MICU for immediate RSI before complete airway loss. Massive Parkland formula fluid. Burns unit with ICU. Prognosis extremely poor (Baux score >120 = near 100% mortality) — palliative discussion may be appropriate.

templates.call_description: Kumbh Mela stampede, Nashik. 52-year-old male found in crowd, pulseless, not breathing. CPR difficult due to crowd. No AED available. Large crowd preventing access. Unknown downtime.
templates.protocol_notes: Crowd event cardiac arrest — Police for crowd clearance urgently. ALS with defibrillator, CPR in place. Trauma from crush mechanism possible. Massive pre-planned event medical coverage failure. Any nearest ED.

templates.call_description: 62-year-old female, liver transplant recipient on tacrolimus, 2-day history of confusion, temperature swings, rigors. No localising symptoms. CMV infection or rejection possible. BP 82/50, HR 122.
templates.protocol_notes: Post-transplant sepsis — immunosuppression makes fever response unreliable. ALS, broad-spectrum IV antibiotics + antifungal cover, blood cultures x3. Contact transplant centre directly. Do NOT stop immunosuppression without transplant team guidance.

templates.call_description: 68-year-old female, fell from bed at night. Right wrist pain and deformity (likely Colles), hip mildly tender but able to weight bear. Alert, GCS 15. BP 145/90, HR 78. On aspirin and metformin.
templates.protocol_notes: Elderly fall — multiple fracture risk on osteoporosis. BLS for transport. Orthopaedic ED for wrist and hip assessment. Fall prevention review. Bone density assessment recommended.

templates.call_description: 53-year-old male, asbestos worker with known mesothelioma, severe dyspnoea at rest. Dull to percussion left chest, trachea shifted right. Pleural effusion on prior X-ray. SpO2 88%, RR 36.
templates.protocol_notes: Massive pleural effusion with mediastinal shift in mesothelioma. ALS — O2, positioning. Emergency thoracentesis or chest drain at hospital. Cardiothoracic + oncology hospital. Palliative care input.

templates.call_description: 9-year-old, Pune summer, playing outside, now sudden right arm weakness and facial droop. History of congenital heart disease (repaired TOF). FAST positive. GCS 13. BP 140/85, HR 100.
templates.protocol_notes: Paediatric arterial ischaemic stroke — rare but devastating. FAST positive. ALS, paediatric stroke protocol — thrombolysis NOT routine in children. Paediatric neurology + cardiac centre. MRI urgently.

templates.call_description: 35-year-old male, acute onset severe right flank pain + fever 40°C + rigors. Urine cloudy. BP 82/48, HR 134. Diabetic. Not responding to oral fluids. Looks septic.
templates.protocol_notes: Emphysematous pyelonephritis in diabetic — gas-forming organism. ALS, sepsis bundle (antibiotics within 1h), IV fluids, blood cultures. Diabetic + urological sepsis = high mortality. Nephrology + urology hospital for emergency nephrectomy or drainage.

templates.call_description: Nagpur orange juice factory ammonia refrigerant leak. 20 workers exposed to ammonia fumes. Severe eye and respiratory irritation throughout factory floor. 4 workers collapsed, one not breathing.
templates.protocol_notes: Ammonia mass toxic inhalation. MICU for critical (apnoeic worker), multiple ALS for collapsed, BLS for ambulatory. PPE Level B minimum. Fire hazmat for decontamination corridor. Toxicology + pulmonology hospital. Bronchodilators + steroids.

templates.call_description: City-wide surge scenario — simultaneous MCIs across Pune: (1) building collapse Hadapsar 15+ trapped, (2) bus accident Katraj 20 casualties, (3) chemical plant fire Ranjangaon 10 workers. All hospitals nearing capacity. Control room requesting state mutual aid.
templates.protocol_notes: STATE SURGE — Task 9 trigger scenario. Declare city-wide surge. Activate mutual aid from Mumbai, Nashik, Aurangabad. Distribute: building collapse → Sassoon, Deenanath; bus accident → Ruby Hall, Jupiter; chemical fire → Burns unit at Sassoon. Contact NDMA. All off-duty staff recalled. Hospital diversions to be managed.
